name: Lobster and Corn Chowder
recipeIngredient: 4 Ears fresh corn (OR 2 cups frozen corn kernels)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preparation: Bring 4 quarts of water to boil in a 6- to 8-quart soup kettle. Add lobster and cover, then boil for about 10 minutes. Remove the shells and carcass into a large saucepan with the half-and-half. Bring to a boil, lower heat, and simmer for 4 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
2. Melt butter in a soup kettle over medium-low heat. Add potatoes, onion, and corn kernels (or thawed frozen corn). Saute until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. Strain the lobster cream over the vegetables, bring to a simmer, and then simmer slowly for 6 to 8 minutes until the potatoes are tender.
4. Stir in lobster meat, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper. Simmer until the lobster meat is hot, about 5 minutes.
5. Ladle into warm bowls and serve with Corn Crisps.
Makes 4 servings.
